title: SynthesisInsightV3
description: Day-level synthesis. Consumes sleep + recovery + activity insights and produces the unified daily verdict, headline, summary, top action, and key insight. Cites use-case-specific KPI values verbatim.

incomplete.description: Single completion flag. true = artifact still in-flight or failed validation. Flipped to false only by the writer as the very last step before atomic rename.

verdict_band.description: Day-level verdict. Aligned with day_score_deterministic threshold (≥65 → above_usual, ≤35 → below_usual, else steady) UNLESS use-case insights strongly disagree (then explain in reasoning of headline/summary).

headline.description: Verb-led day headline. Cites the dominant signal across domains.

summary_short.description: Mobile day summary, ≤120 chars. Must mention at least one concrete value across domains.

summary_long.description: Desktop day summary, ≤240 chars. Must reference at least 2 domain KPI values.

key_insight.description: One-sentence cross-domain insight. The non-obvious connection (e.g. high training load → low RMSSD → poor sleep). Must cite ≥2 values from different domains.

top_action_today.description: Single highest-priority action across all domains. Picked from use-case suggestions (cite source_domain) or composed from cross-domain signal.

reasoning.description: Why this is THE top action across all domains. Reference the signals.

domain_pointers.description: One pointer per domain (sleep, recovery, activity) — surfaces the most salient KPI/finding from each use case for drill-down navigation.

reasoning.description: Why this KPI is the salient one for this domain today.

callout.description: One-line salient hook for the drill-down (cites a concrete number).

contradictions.description: Detected conflicts between use-case suggestions (e.g. sleep says 'rest', activity says 'train'). Empty array if none.

conflict.description: What's the conflict in plain words.

resolution.description: Which side wins and why. Cite the determining signal.

reasoning.description: Why this confidence: averaged from per-domain confidences, adjusted for cross-domain coherence.
